ORDER ON DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS CRUNCH LLC, NEFC CRUNCH LLC AND AGT AQUISITION WIND DOWN LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, THIRD, FOURTH, SIXTH, AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND, FIFTH, AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO ALLEGE, IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, FACTS SUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE PLAINTIFF DID NOT RECEIVE UNINTERRUPTED AND UNRESTRICTED MEAL BREAKS AND/ OR REST PERIODS. AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO ALLEGE, IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, FACTS SUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE PLAINTIFF DID NOT RECEIVE WAGES UPON HER TERMINATION. AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO ALLEGE THAT THE LABOR AND WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT AGENCY NOTIFIED THE PARTIES THAT IT DID NOT INTEND TO INVESTIGATE. THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED. (OPPOSING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO SEND BY WAY OF E-MAIL TO THE COURT WITH A COPY TO THE PREVAILING PARTY.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS CRUNCH LLC, NEFC CRUNCH LLC, AND AGT CRUNCH ACQUISTION WIND DOWN LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE ROTHBERG'S PRAYERS FOR RELIEF IN THE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS TO SUPPORT THE CLASS ALLEGATIONS AND THE PRAYERS FOR DECLARATORY AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. (OPPOSING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND SEND BY WAY OF E-MAIL TO THE COURT WITH A COPY TO THE PREVAILING PARTY) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-24-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CRUNCH LLC'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. THE INCLUSION OF THE RELEASE OF FLSA CLAIMS IN THE PROPOSED CLASS SETTLEMENT EXCEEDS THE SCOPE OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE PROPOSED NOTICE IS INSUFFICIENT AS IT DOES NOT APPRISE THE PUTATIVE CLASS MEMBERS OF THE EXISTENCE OF THE FEDERAL ACTION INVOLVING FLSA CLAIMS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CRUNCH LLC'S Joint Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement - To be heard in Department 613 before Judge Goldsmith. The parties are to meet and confer and contact the clerk at (415) 551-3840 to set the hearing date. The court will not provide a court reporter for this hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prelim Approval Of Class Action Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-09-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MOLLY A. DESARIO & ATTY. JESSICA L. CAMPBELL OF SCOTT COLE & ASSOCAITES, TEL. NO. (510) 891-9800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARK D. KEMPLE OF THE GREENBERG TAURIG LLP, TEL. NO. (310) 586-7864 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. ALISON GIBBS OF HUNT ORTMANN, TEL. NO. (626) 440-5200 APPEARING FOR PUTATIVE CLASS MEMBER OSABEMI-YI ADEDAPOIDLE- TYEHIMBA.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CRUNCH LLC's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement is Denied. The inclusion of the release of FLSA claims in the proposed class settlement exceeds the scope of the Second Amended Complaint. The proposed notice is insufficient as it does not apprise the putative class members of the existence of the Federal action involving FLSA claims. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-18-2012 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS CRUNCH LLC, NEFC CRUNCH LLC and AGT AQUISITION WIND DOWN LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Off calendar per stipulation and order filed on December 12, 2012. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-21-2012 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARK D. KEMPLE OF THE GREENBERG/TRAURIG LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (310) 586-7864 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CRUNCH LLC, NEFC CRUNCH LLC AND AGT AQUISITION WIND DOWN LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled as to the first, third, fourth, sixth, and seventh causes of action and sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the second, fifth, and eighth causes of action. As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to allege, if she can do so in good faith, facts sufficient to demonstrate Plaintiff did not receive uninterrupted and unrestricted meal breaks and/ or rest periods. As to the fifth cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to allege, if she can do so in good faith, facts sufficient to demonstrate Plaintiff did not receive wages upon her termination. As to the eighth cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to allege that the Labor and Workforce Development Agency notified the parties that it did not intend to investigate. The other causes of action are adequately alleged. (Opposing party to prepare a form of order and to send by way of e-mail to the court with a copy to the prevailing party.) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. =302/HK

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CRUNCH LLC, NEFC CRUNCH LLC, AND AGT CRUNCH ACQUISTION WIND DOWN LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE ROTHBERG'S PRAYERS FOR RELIEF IN THE COMPLAINT IS Denied. The complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support the class allegations and the prayers for declaratory and injunctive relief. (Opposing party to prepare a form of order and send by way of e-mail to the court with a copy to the prevailing party) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. =302/HK
